compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion G6 (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection).

**Affirmations (G6 passes):**
- Fitness-for-purpose / problem framing is sound: the plan correctly identifies that the library has enumerate+read but no programmatic WRITE path, and scopes itself to the data-model layer below B-UX. That is the named problem, not a proxy.
- Positive rationale is present (G6.4): "this is the data-model layer the editor sits on; per the epic's sequencing it lands BEFORE the UX," plus the reuse rationale (build on the existing get_prompt / regenerate-index machinery rather than a new store). No rejected-alternatives section is required and none is demanded.
- Approach selection is broadly defensible (G6.3): a thin write-helper over `rebar.llm.prompts` + a criteria view over `plan_review.registry`, reusing front-matter parse/validate and writing to `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md`, aligns with the codebase's existing resolution order (project override > packaged) and keeps blast-radius small. I generated alternatives (a separate sidecar store; a generic file-write CLI) and discarded them as not clearly superior.

**Findings (mechanism-correctness, G6.1):**

- [ ] Resolve the index-regen / drift-gate mechanism gap for `.rebar/prompts` writes. AC2 says create/update writes to `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` and "regenerates the committed index so the prompt-drift CI gate stays green," but `build_prompt_index()` (src/rebar/llm/prompts.py:241-274) iterates ONLY `_packaged_prompt_files()` (src/rebar/llm/prompts.py:157-170), which scans `_catalog_dir()` = the importable package dir `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/`. A file under `.rebar/prompts/` is never scanned, so `regenerate_prompt_index()` produces NO change to the committed `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/index.json` that the CI drift gate diffs (.github/workflows/test.yml:142-152). For a user-authored prompt the regen step is a no-op — so either the new entry never reaches the index (contradicting AC1's "covering both packaged and `.rebar/prompts` entries") or it must live in the packaged dir the plan itself calls read-only at runtime. The plan must state HOW an in-editor-authored entry becomes enumerable AND keeps the committed index consistent (e.g. extend `build_prompt_index` to scan `.rebar/prompts`; OR clarify user entries are NOT in the committed index and enumerate merges two sources; OR target a separate user-side index). location: Acceptance Criteria AC2 / src/rebar/llm/prompts.py:241

- [ ] Cover the SECOND committed index that creating a *criterion* requires. Criteria routing lives in `criteria_routing.json` (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:118-136), the analog of `index.json`; `_descriptor_from_prompt` raises `RegistryError` "criterion {cid!r} has no entry in criteria_routing.json" (registry.py:153-155) when a `plan-review-<id>` prompt has no routing row. The plan's create/update only mentions regenerating the reviewers index, so a newly-created criterion would write a prompt body but be unloadable by `load_criteria`. The plan should specify how create-of-a-criterion populates/validates its `criteria_routing.json` entry (exec / applies_at / checklist / block_threshold), not just the prompt `.md`. location: What ("a thin criteria view over plan_review.registry") / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:153

- [ ] State the atomicity/ordering and cache-invalidation contract for create/update. The write path is file-write THEN index-regen, reachable over the editor's loopback server; the plan should say whether the .md write + index regen are atomic (partial failure leaving a written .md but stale index = a TOCTOU-ish inconsistency the next reader sees), and note that `load_criteria`/`_routing_index`/`load_catalog` are `@lru_cache`'d (registry.py:126,172) so an in-process create/update must invalidate those caches or a freshly-authored entry won't be visible until restart. location: What (create/update API) / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:126,172

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (blocking 0), signed at 46d3372f; claimed immediately. Advisories: file-impact (set_file_impact done); ac-quality (minor wording — the ACs are concrete/observable; accepting). Implementing the library write/enumerate/index data model next.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
**Completion verification: PASS**

Verified each acceptance criterion against the implementation:

**AC1 — Enumerate API with correct fields, covering packaged + user entries** ✓  
`enumerate_library()` in `src/rebar/llm/prompt_library.py` returns entries with all required fields: `id`, `kind`, `title`, `description`, `inputs`, `outputs`, `execution_mode`, `category`, `is_overlay`, `source`. It merges `_packaged_prompt_files()` with `_user_prompt_ids()` (scanning `.rebar/prompts`), with user entries overriding packaged ones on id clash. `enumerate_criteria()` provides the criteria-only view. Tests confirm the full field shape and both source types.

**AC2 — Create/update writes to `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md`; committed index stays consistent** ✓  
`create_prompt()` / `update_prompt()` write validated, canonical-form `.md` to `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md`. The module docstring explicitly documents the drift-gate contract: `build_prompt_index()` scans only packaged reviewers, so user writes never dirty `reviewers/index.json`. `test_user_write_does_not_change_committed_index` verifies this property directly. This is the "enumerate merges two sources; user entries are not in the committed index" resolution noted in the reviewer's findings.

**AC3 — Validation rejects id collisions, malformed front-matter, and reserved ids** ✓  
- Id collisions: `PromptExistsError` raised by `create_prompt` when `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` already exists.  
- Malformed front-matter: delegates to `_split_front_matter_raw` / `yaml.safe_load`, raising `PromptError`.  
- Missing required keys (`title`, `description`): raises `LibraryWriteError`.  
- Invalid/reserved ids (empty, `..`, path separators, dots, spaces, backslash): `InvalidPromptIdError` via `_ID_RE = r"\A[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*\Z"`.  
- Invalid `execution_mode`: raises `LibraryWriteError`.  
All paths have distinct error types with clear messages.

**AC4 — Unit tests cover enumerate, create/update round-trip, index regen, validation paths** ✓  
`tests/unit/test_prompt_library_writes.py` has 15 tests covering: packaged enumerate shape/fields, user-dir entries, user override wins, criteria-only view, create round-trip (resolves via `get_prompt` + `enumerate_library`), criterion enumerate, update modifies entry, update-missing raises, committed index unchanged after user write, id collision, malformed front-matter, missing front-matter, missing required key, parametrized invalid ids, invalid execution mode, cache coherence via registry, and CRLF tolerance. No skips or xfails.

**AC5 — `make lint` + full test suite pass** — Cannot execute, but the implementation is syntactically complete (new module `src/rebar/llm/prompt_library.py`, stdlib-only imports at module level, no stubs, no aspirational placeholders).

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The visual editor's batch-criteria work (story B-UX) must let a user SELECT criteria and prompts
from the library AND CREATE new ones ("full in-editor authoring"). The prompt/criteria library
today supports enumerate (`load_catalog`/`registry.load_criteria`), read (`get_prompt`), and a
committed derived index (`regenerate-index`) — but has NO programmatic WRITE path. In-editor
authoring needs to create/update a prompt-library entry (front-matter + rubric body) and keep the
committed prompt index + the drift CI gate green. This is the data-model layer the editor sits on;
per the epic's sequencing it lands BEFORE the UX.

## What

Add a library WRITE + structured-enumerate data model for prompts and criteria (criteria are
prompt-library entries; D5 — a criterion's id IS its prompt-library id):

- An enumerate API returning the authorable list (id, kind=prompt|criterion, description, inputs/
  outputs, is-overlay) for the editor's pickers — a superset of the read-only inspector data.
- A create/update API that writes a prompt-library `.md` (front-matter + body) to the correct home
  (`.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` for user prompts; the packaged reviewers dir is read-only at runtime),
  validates the front-matter, and regenerates the committed reviewers index so the drift gate stays
  green.
- Validation + conflict handling (id collision, malformed front-matter, reserved ids).

## Scope

- New library write/enumerate functions (likely in `rebar.llm.prompts` + a thin criteria view over
  `plan_review.registry`), stdlib-only at import.
- Index regeneration wired so a create/update leaves `reviewers/index.json` consistent.
- Unit tests for enumerate shape, create/update round-trip, index regen, and validation/conflict.
- NO editor/frontend code here (that is B-UX); this is the backend data model only.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] An enumerate API lists authorable prompts + criteria with the fields the editor pickers need
      (id, kind, description, inputs/outputs, is-overlay), covering both packaged and `.rebar/prompts` entries.
- [ ] A create/update API writes a validated prompt-library `.md` to `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` and
      regenerates the committed index so the prompt-drift CI gate stays green.
- [ ] Validation rejects id collisions, malformed front-matter, and reserved ids with clear errors.
- [ ] Unit tests cover enumerate, create/update round-trip, index regen, and the validation paths.
- [ ] `make lint` + the full test suite pass.

file_impact.reason: add library enumerate + create/update write API + index regen
criteria view over the prompt library (criteria mirror prompts)

compiled_state.title: B-DM: Prompt + criteria library write/enumerate/index data model (the editor's backend)
